channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: MiG-29K Set for Major Upgrade as Navy Plans Uttam AESA Integration
item.description: The Indian Navy has moved decisively to upgrade its MiG-29K carrier-based fighter fleet, initiating a major program to replace the aging Zhuk-ME mechanically scanned radar with an indigenous Uttam Mk2 AESA radar developed by DRDO’s Electronics and Radar Development Establishment (LRDE).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AVNL’s Turret Upgrade Push: HVF Avadi Floats Key EOI for Electromagnetic Traverse Systems in T-90 and T-72 Tanks
item.description: Armoured Vehicles Nigam Limited (AVNL), through its flagship Heavy Vehicles Factory (HVF) in Avadi, has issued a critical Expression of Interest (EOI) that signals a deeper technological overhaul of India’s armored fleet. The tender, titled “Expression of Interest for Turret Traversing Mechanism Electro Magnet Assembly,” under Tender ID 2026_AVNL_273418_1, is not just a component-level procurement but a foundational step toward modernizing turret actuation systems on frontline tanks such as the T-90 Bhishma and T-72 Ajeya.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GTRE Seeks Industry Partner for High Energy Ignition System: A Critical Step Toward Indigenous Aero-Engine Capability
item.description: India’s push for full-spectrum self-reliance in aero-engine technology has taken a decisive step forward, with the Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) issuing an Expression of Interest (EoI) to identify an Indian industry partner for the development and production of a “Ready-to-Fit” High Energy Ignition (HEI) Unit along with its associated high-tension ignition cables. The move targets one of the most critical yet often overlooked subsystems in modern jet engines—an element that directly determines engine reliability, safety, and combat survivability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India to Issue Rafale RFP to Dassault as 114-Fighter Deal Moves Ahead with 50% Indigenous Production
item.description: India is set to formally issue a Request for Proposal (RFP) to French aerospace major Dassault Aviation by mid-next month, marking a critical step forward in one of the Indian Air Force’s most significant fighter acquisition programs. The move follows the Defence Acquisition Council’s clearance of the deal on February 12, paving the way for detailed contract negotiations once the RFP is formally released.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Hunt for the Erieye: How IAF Fine-Tunes SEAD/DEAD Mastery with R-37M Missiles After S-400’s Historic 314 km Kill
item.description: Following the historic shootdown of a Pakistani Saab 2000 Erieye at a staggering 314 km range by the Indian Air Force’s S-400 Triumf during Operation Sindoor in May 2025, the IAF has unlocked a decisive tactical edge that is reshaping its Suppression and Destruction of Enemy Air Defenses (SEAD/DEAD) playbook. The loss of this high-value airborne early warning and control (AEW&C) platform robbed the Pakistan Air Force of its prized “God’s eye view,” instantly carving massive radar blind spots and triggering widespread communication breakdowns across the enemy’s network-centric operations. What was once a formidable force multiplier has become a glaring vulnerability that Indian planners are now systematically exploiting.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dynamatic Technologies Developing Indigenous Stabilised EO/IR Payload with 20 km Detection Range for ISR Platforms
item.description: Dynamatic Technologies is moving deeper into the intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) domain with the development of an in-house stabilised electro-optical/infrared (EO/IR) payload, signalling a strategic shift from pure aerostructures manufacturing to high-value mission systems. The company claims that the new sensor ball will offer a detection range of up to 20 kilometres, positioning it within the competitive bracket of medium-to-long-range ISR payloads typically deployed on UAVs, helicopters, and light surveillance aircraft.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Military Drone Exports Poised for Explosive Multifold Growth After 2025 Pakistan Conflict
item.description: The May 2025 clash between India and Pakistan — widely described as the world’s first “drone war” between nuclear-armed neighbors — showcased the effectiveness of indigenous Indian drones in real combat conditions. Companies such as Solar Industries, NewSpace Research and Technologies, and Zen Technologies saw their systems deployed extensively, boosting their international credibility as reliable, low-cost alternatives.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi on four-day visit to Myanmar to boost maritime cooperation
item.description: During the visit, the Navy Chief will hold bilateral discussions with senior Myanmar leadership, including General Ye Win Oo, Commander-in-Chief of the Myanmar Armed Forces, General U Htun Aung, Defence Minister of Myanmar, and Admiral Htein Win, Commander-in-Chief of the Myanmar Navy, according to an official statement.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: CBI Coordinates Return Of Fugitive Aalok Kumar From UAE
item.description: According to the CBI, Aalok Kumar was wanted in connection with a case registered by Haryana Police for offences including cheating, forgery, criminal conspiracy and use of forged documents for obtaining passports fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Government launches indigenous Cell Broadcast System for instant disaster alerts
item.description: In a social media post, Union Minister for Communications Jyotiraditya Scindia said that if people receive a message like this on their mobiles, there is no need to panic as this is a test message from India’s new mobile-based disaster communication system. He said, it has been developed by Department of Telecommunications in collaboration with National Disaster Management Authority using cell broadcast technology. Mr Scindia said, in the future, through this same system, instant alerts can be sent in the local language and according to the location in the event of a disaster or emergency.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Mizoram’s Last Militant Group Lays Down Arms, State Declared Insurgency-Free
item.description: The Sanate faction was considered the last surviving insurgent group in the northeastern state.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Dawood Aide Salim Dola Lived As ‘Hamza’ In Turkey On Bulgarian Passport
item.description: On Thursday, a local court in Mumbai sent Dola, 59, to the custody of the Narcotics Control Bureau till May 8. Officials privy to the developments said initial probe found Dola was staying in Turkiye on a Bulgarian passport under the name ‘Hamza’.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: 3 DRG jawans injured as IED goes off during demining operation in Chhattisgarh
item.description: This was the first blast incident linked to Naxalite activity since the state was declared free from armed Maoists on March 31.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Security forces destroy 23 illegal bunkers, seize 18 IEDs in Manipur
item.description: A 12-bore pump action gun, 17 cartridges of different calibres and 111 empty cases of fired bullets were also seized.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Farooq Abdullah claims lack of support within INDIA bloc on restoration of J-K statehood
item.description: He also flagged the lack of a robust, unified approach to Jammu and Kashmir’s issues as a primary point of contention within the bloc
item.dc:creator: PTI
